design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate overhead map to locate Jaws, trigger side-view diving encounters with smaller enemies, collect conch shells as currency, upgrade attack power at ports, eventually corner and defeat Jaws through multi-phase combat
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (conch shells lost on death) balanced against progressive power gains; spatial awareness of Jaws' proximity via beeping receiver creates hunt/hunted dynamic
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Being a shark hunter equipped with increasingly powerful weapons (depth charges, torpedoes, strobe device); transition from vulnerable diver to capable boat captain delivering final blow
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Rejects pure action prowess in favor of economic/resource management (conch shell trading) and patience-based final boss design requiring precise timing rather than reflexes; bonus game directly mimics Galaga formation patterns, suggesting intentional genre blending

difficulty.lives_system: Extra life awarded per 30,000 points; death penalties include power level drop, loss of tracking device, loss of half accumulated conch shells, but full Jaws health replenishment
difficulty.checkpointing: No checkpoints within encounters; map state persists between encounters but loss conditions reset Jaws' health fully
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency: players control which ports to visit (early receiver vs. attack power upgrades), which weapon to equip (diver vs. submarine), submarine fragility is known tradeoff; final boss requires timing mastery rather than pattern memorization, with acknowledged randomness in Jaws' breach movements

progression.upgrade_system: Conch shell currency system: first port visit unlocks receiver (Jaws proximity detector), subsequent visits increase attack power incrementally; submarine available as fragile alternative weapon with torpedoes and depth charges; crabs provide temporary diver speed boost
progression.unlock_mechanism: Receiver unlocked after first port visit (mandatory); submarine appears at random map locations; bonus Galaga-style minigame triggered after killing specific number of smaller sharks; strobe device available for final phase only
replayability_hooks: Bonus game difficulty varies with jellyfish formation destruction skill
Random submarine placement encourages map exploration
Jaws' random movement patterns in final phase create non-deterministic outcomes
Conch shell economy creates multiple upgrade paths (prioritize receiver early vs. attack power)

world_building_techniques: Overhead map suggests large open hunting area with two ports (economic hubs)
Enemy variety (jellyfish, rays, crabs, smaller sharks) implies ecological food chain
Dorsal fin visual on map creates implicit threat presence before encounters
Title screen music based on John Williams' Jaws score anchors film franchise connection
Music volume fluctuations designed to mimic wave undulation, reinforcing aquatic setting

boss_design.philosophy: Three-phase escalation: initial side-view melee with depth charges/torpedoes where Jaws always collides and releases diver; health-depletion phase requiring sustained damage output; final first-person phase demanding precision timing and spatial judgment (strobe device triggers breach, player must jab bow at exact distance during random lateral/forward/backward movements)
notable_bosses: Jaws (multi-phase): side-view combat → health bar depletion → first-person precision finale with strobe device and random movement, acknowledged as 'quite difficult' requiring 'much patience'

faction_indicators: Two competing ports suggest port allegiance choice (though gameplay does not appear to differentiate)
Shark hunter identity vs. natural world ecosystem (diver perspective)
danger_signals: Jaws proximity indicator (beeping receiver) intensity signals threat escalation
Dorsal fin appearance on map creates hunt initiation moment
Smaller shark encounters establish predatory ecosystem danger before Jaws arrival
Health penalties (power level drop, equipment loss) create visible progression reversal on death
environmental_storytelling: Ocean ecosystem variety (jellyfish, rays, crabs, sharks) establishes food chain hierarchy
Two-port system suggests human settlement/civilization resisting shark threat
Overhead map perspective implies large hunting territory to patrol
Submarine upgrade availability hints at technological escalation arms race

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Jaws NES article)
